title: Framework Registry: PM strategy and prioritization

frameworks.name: Market jobs and barriers
frameworks.summary: Use when the PM needs a compact view of buyer jobs, switching friction, and adoption blockers.
applicability_rules: Use when the wedge is still being defined and switching friction matters more than precise revenue math.
Prefer before broad category claims or platform-level positioning.
evidence_expectations: Need at least one market signal and one operator or customer signal.
Keep one explicit switching-cost or proof barrier in view.
default_when: The category is moving quickly and buyers need trust and proof.
avoid_when: The primary question is precise TAM sizing.

frameworks.name: Customer pain and adoption split
frameworks.summary: Use when the PM must separate support burden from healthy adoption momentum.
applicability_rules: Use when support and adoption evidence should influence priority directly.
Prefer when feedback quality is mixed and needs clustering.
evidence_expectations: Need at least one pain cluster and one adoption cluster.
Keep evidence refs visible for each cluster.
default_when: The PM needs to decide whether to harden, expand, or reopen upstream artifacts.
avoid_when: No customer or operator evidence exists yet.

frameworks.name: Competitive displacement map
frameworks.summary: Use when ProductOS needs a direct view of displacement barriers and taxonomy across real alternatives.
applicability_rules: Use when named competitors, status quo, and in-house alternatives all matter.
Prefer when the PM must justify a challenger wedge.
evidence_expectations: Need named competitors or an explicit status-quo fallback.
Each competitor should carry at least one evidence ref.
default_when: The PM must explain how ProductOS is different without claiming broad platform parity.
avoid_when: Only internal assumptions exist and no bounded comparison can be made.

frameworks.name: Signal-weighted priority
frameworks.summary: Use when ProductOS must rank work by customer value, strategic fit, leverage, and burden.
applicability_rules: Use when one problem, opportunity, or feature must be chosen explicitly.
Prefer when PM override should be preserved rather than hidden.
evidence_expectations: Keep source artifact ids on every ranked option.
Make agentic leverage and compliance burden visible.
default_when: The next bounded slice must be justified to PM and delivery reviewers.
avoid_when: There is only one option and no prioritization decision is actually being made.

selection_policies.selection_logic: Start with market jobs and barriers when trust, switching friction, and buyer proof are more important than precise market math.
Use customer pain and adoption split when support and adoption signals both shape the next priority decision.
Use competitive displacement map when the PM must explain named alternatives, status quo, and in-house risk together.
Use signal-weighted priority whenever ProductOS needs an explicit ranked next move with PM-visible tradeoffs.
